task_completion.description: Verify the agent successfully logged into OpenMRS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenMRS home dashboard is visible, showing the user is logged in (typically indicating 'admin' in the profile/header area).

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the drug management section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser displays the 'Manage Drugs', 'Concept Dictionary', or 'Add Drug' page within the OpenMRS interface.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent entered the correct drug name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Aspirin 500mg ES' is visible inside the 'Name' input field on the drug creation form.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent linked the correct concept.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The concept 'Aspirin' is typed into or selected in the 'Concept' search/input field on the drug creation form.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent entered the correct strength.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The value '500' is visible inside the 'Strength' input field on the drug creation form.

task_completion.description: Verify the new drug entry was successfully saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message (e.g., 'Drug saved') is displayed, or 'Aspirin 500mg ES' is visible as a saved entry in the system's list of drugs.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the web browser to interact with OpenMRS.
integrity.visual_evidence: The actions take place within a standard web browser interface showing the OpenMRS application, not via terminal commands, scripts, or API testing tools.

integrity.description: Verify the agent filled out the genuine OpenMRS form.
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs are entered into the actual OpenMRS web application fields, rather than being typed into a text editor, notepad, or unrelated application.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use developer tools to fake the result.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (like the Elements or Console tabs) are not open and being used to alter the page's HTML to fake a success message or drug list.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected drug name to be created is exactly 'Aspirin 500mg ES'. The expected concept to link to this drug is 'Aspirin'. The expected strength value is '500'.
